section: JEF P106 – The Making of a Scientist
title: The Making of a Scientist – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: Robert W. Peterson

questions.question: Who is Richard Ebright?
questions.correct_answer: A young scientist who achieved success through curiosity and hard work
questions.explanation: Richard Ebright is the central character whose scientific journey forms the theme of the prose.

questions.question: What first sparked Richard Ebright’s interest in science?
questions.explanation: His fascination with butterflies opened the door to scientific exploration.

questions.question: Who played a major role in encouraging Ebright’s learning?
questions.explanation: Ebright’s mother provided emotional support, resources, and motivation.

questions.question: Why did Richard Ebright fail at his first science fair?
questions.correct_answer: He presented a display instead of a real experiment
questions.explanation: He realised that true science involves experimentation, not decoration.

questions.question: What lesson did Ebright learn from his failure?
questions.correct_answer: Real science requires experimentation and inquiry
questions.explanation: Failure taught him to focus on genuine scientific investigation.

questions.question: What was Ebright’s important discovery related to monarch butterflies?
questions.correct_answer: The function of gold spots on the monarch pupa
questions.explanation: He discovered that the spots produce a hormone essential for development.

questions.question: Which qualities helped Ebright become a successful scientist?
questions.explanation: These qualities guided his continuous growth and achievements.

questions.question: How does the lesson define a true scientist?
questions.correct_answer: One who combines curiosity with disciplined effort
questions.explanation: The prose emphasises attitude and approach over mere intelligence.

questions.question: What role did failure play in Ebright’s success?
questions.correct_answer: It motivated him to improve and work harder
questions.explanation: Failure became a turning point rather than a setback.

questions.question: What message does the lesson convey to students?
questions.correct_answer: Curiosity and hard work are essential for success
questions.explanation: The story inspires students to learn actively and persistently.

notes.title: Scientific Curiosity
notes.content: Curiosity is shown as the starting point of all scientific discovery.

notes.title: Learning from Failure
notes.content: Failure helps identify mistakes and improves future performance.

facts.fact: Richard Ebright began his scientific journey as a butterfly collector.
Ebright’s experiments led to international recognition.
